data.title: CHAPTER 8 — PROMPTS FOR WRITING AND EDITING
blocks: After this chapter you will be able to:
Chapter 8 — Prompts for Writing and Edit — Distinguish between drafting help, restructuring, line editing, and tone adaptation — and prompt for each separately Ask

data.title: THE WRITING OWNERSHIP PROBLEM.
blocks: When you ask Claude to "improve" your writing without constraints, two things happen that you may not notice:
The Writing Ownership Problem — Voice drift: The prose gradually becomes more confident, more polished, and more generic. Claude's statistical average v

data.title: THE WRITING TASK TAXONOMY.
blocks: Diagnose — Nothing — assesses and lists
Restructure — Order, organization, headings
Line edit — Sentence clarity, word choice
Tone adaptation — Register, formality, audience fit

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — BEFORE / AFTER: A WRITING PROM

data.title: BEFORE / AFTER: A WRITING PROMPT WALKTHROUGH
blocks: · Do not add any claims or findings not present in my draft
· Do not remove or soften any hedge language (e.g., "may suggest,"
Do not change the four-part structure background / purpose /
· Keep all reported statistics exactly as written
· Word count of your version

data.title: THE DIAGNOSE-FIRST PATTERN.
blocks: Clarity Can a reader who is unfamiliar with this project understand
Hedge accuracy Do the hedges in the findings section match the
Structure Does the order of sections follow the logic of the argument?

S08.meta: // SLIDE 08 — AI DISCLOSURE IN WRITING CONTE

data.title: AI DISCLOSURE IN WRITING CONTEXTS.
blocks: · Check your institution's current policy on AI-assisted writing
· Check your target publication or submission context's policy
· Document which parts of your work involved AI assistance if disclosure is required
Understand that "Claude wrote it" is not a defense for an inaccurate claim you submitted it

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Asking for "improvements" without naming which kind.
Not asking for a change log on line edits.
Letting voice drift accumulate across passes.
Using the output without reading the full text.
Accepting hedges that weakened.

data.title: THE EDITING PROMPT CHECKLIST.
blocks: Named which task type (diagnose / restructure / line edit / tone / draft) — Mixed tasks create mixed constraints
Stated preservation constraints explicitly — Unconstrained edits drift
Requested a change log or markup — Review is only possible if changes are visible
Stated authorial ownership — Sets the standard for evaluating the output
